description: AI consultancy fit scoring rubric — Aixer Solutions Singapore SME targeting

revenue_stage.description: Can they afford a SGD 3K–25K AI consultancy engagement?

scoring_guide.0.9: SGD 1M–5M revenue — strong SME budget, decision-maker has authority
scoring_guide.0.7: SGD 300K–1M revenue — moderate budget, phased pilot approach needed
scoring_guide.0.3: SGD 100K–300K revenue — tight budget, revisit in 6–12 months
scoring_guide.0.1: Above SGD 5M — outside target SME range

tech_maturity.description: Can they adopt and integrate AI solutions without prohibitive change management?

scoring_guide.1.0: Actively hiring AI/ML engineers or data scientists — tech-first culture
scoring_guide.0.85: Modern cloud stack (AWS/GCP/Azure), hiring general tech roles
scoring_guide.0.7: Has internal IT team, some cloud adoption, basic digital ops
scoring_guide.0.4: Legacy systems dominant, IT is mostly support function
scoring_guide.0.2: No visible tech investment in last 2 years — high change risk

automation_readiness.description: Do they have clear process pain points AI can solve within 6–8 weeks?

scoring_guide.1.0: Operational headcount growing faster than revenue — textbook automation case
scoring_guide.0.95: Public complaints (Glassdoor, news) about process speed or errors
scoring_guide.0.85: High operational volume with no visible automation — obvious gap
scoring_guide.0.6: Some automation but clear gaps in handoff or exception handling
scoring_guide.0.3: Already heavily automated — limited net new opportunity

customer_ai_readiness.description: Is customer-facing AI (chatbot, recommendation, personalisation) relevant?

scoring_guide.1.0: E-commerce or SaaS — direct customer AI applicability at scale
scoring_guide.0.95: B2C with high customer volume (1,000+ customers)
scoring_guide.0.7: B2B2C or mixed model with end-consumer touchpoints
scoring_guide.0.4: Pure B2B with small customer count (<100 accounts)
scoring_guide.0.2: Government-facing or internal-only — customer AI not applicable

multi_agent_fit.description: Do they need coordinated AI workflows across departments or processes?

scoring_guide.1.0: Cross-departmental workflows with clear handoff pain — ideal multi-agent case
scoring_guide.0.95: Supply chain or logistics requiring real-time coordination
scoring_guide.0.8: Operations + customer service + finance touchpoints all active
scoring_guide.0.4: Single department automation only — multi-agent overkill
scoring_guide.0.15: Single simple use case — chatbot or report generator

red_flags.description: Company has recently deployed a competing AI solution
Under 10 employees or estimated revenue under SGD 300K
Sells exclusively to Singapore government — long procurement cycles
red_flags.action: NURTURE — revisit when govt AI spend opens

green_flags.description: Founder/CEO posting about operational challenges on LinkedIn
Raised funding in last 12 months — capital available
Main competitor has announced AI adoption — urgency created
Hiring 20%+ headcount in operational/support roles — classic scaling pain
Announced new market entry, product line, or facility

PASS.action: Skip — not a fit at this stage

FOLLOW_UP.action: Send outreach — may need warm intro or longer nurture

URGENT.action: Reach out today — high fit, time-sensitive
